enrichment.summary: Kyle hosts the first episode of 'News with Kyle' at 1:30 AM while high, scrolling through the Huffington Post and riffing on current stories. He covers US Middle East policy, Russia's role in Syria, the 2016 Republican primary, the Supreme Court vacancy, North Korea, Greece's pension crisis, and Pope Francis criticizing Mexico's wealthy elite, weaving in broader takes on capitalism vs. socialism and populism.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.topic: US involvement in the Middle East
stances.position: should stay out entirely; Russia has historical and geographic claim to that sphere
Russia wants peace there; let them handle it instead of spending US money
won't last more than five years due to internal instability
too brash, will make bad conservative appointments, Republicans are scared of him
his tax-the-rich policies will create a more meritocratic, competitive society
dying because it appeals to people and power structures that no longer exist
Obama must nominate; Rubio's pledge to block is a direct violation of checks and balances
socialism is better because people can vote out bad decision-makers; corporations cannot be voted out

stances.topic: Pope Francis criticizing Mexico's wealthy elite
stances.position: correct; wealth inequality must be addressed by investing in people
should be government-run so citizens can vote out bad management
